audit_status.source_base: Greek Romans text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for living sacrifice, transformed mind, body gifts, sincere love, blessing enemies, overcoming harm with good.

verses.restored: Therefore I urge you, siblings, through the mercies of the Divine, to present your bodies as living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to the Divine, your reasonable/spiritual service.
And do not be conformed to this age, but be transformed by the renewal of the mind, so that you may discern what is the will of the Divine: the good, pleasing, and complete.
For through the grace given to me I say to everyone among you not to think beyond what one ought to think, but to think into sober-mindedness, as the Divine apportioned to each a measure of trust.
For just as in one body we have many members, and all the members do not have the same practice,
so we, the many, are one body in Messiah, and individually members of one another.
Having gifts differing according to the grace given to us: if prophecy, according to the proportion of trust;
if service, in the service; if the one teaching, in the teaching;
if the one encouraging, in encouragement; the one sharing, in simplicity/generosity; the one leading, in diligence; the one showing mercy, in cheerfulness.
Let love be unhypocritical; detest the harmful; cling to the good.
In sibling-love be devoted to one another; in honor, leading the way for one another;
in diligence, not shrinking; in spirit, boiling; serving the Lord;
rejoicing in hope, enduring in pressure, persevering in prayer;
sharing in the needs of the holy ones; pursuing hospitality.
Bless those pursuing you; bless and do not curse.
Rejoice with those rejoicing; weep with those weeping.
Think the same toward one another; do not think high things, but be carried with the lowly. Do not become wise in yourselves.
Repay no one harm for harm; give thought beforehand to beautiful/good things before all humans.
If possible, as far as it depends on you, live in peace with all humans.
Beloved, do not avenge yourselves, but give place to wrath; for it is written: Vengeance is mine, I will repay, says the Lord.
But if your enemy hungers, feed that one; if thirsty, give drink; for doing this you will heap coals of fire upon that one's head.
Do not be conquered by harm, but conquer harm with good.

translation_notes: Bodies are presented positively as living sacrifice; this is embodied worship, not body-denial.
Romans 12 moves from doctrine to embodied communal practice: gifts, hospitality, empathy, enemy-love, and non-retaliation.
